project.name: The Playroom
project.summary: Adults-only, privacy-first CNM/lifestyle platform combining social discovery, compatibility matching, encrypted messaging, events/clubs, and a curated marketplace for sex shops.
productAxes: Profiles and social discovery for singles, couples, clubs, and shops
Matching based on kink-test-derived tags, limits, and compatibility scoring
Events and clubs with reservations, VIP priority, and delayed location reveal
Curated marketplace for sex shops with Stripe Connect onboarding and moderated catalog

steps: Date of birth collection and 18+ gate
onboarding.currentGap: The UI sends accountType, ageVerifiedAt, onboardingComplete, and dateOfBirth to PATCH /api/users/me, but the current handler only updates display_name.

matching.algorithm: Simplified Jaccard-style similarity over positive tags after eliminating hard-limit incompatibilities
matching.freeTierBehavior: Randomized feed with daily match cap
matching.vipBehavior: Compatibility-ranked feed and compatibility endpoint access

messaging.delivery: Persist to DB via /api/messages and broadcast realtime events via Ably

eventsAndClubs.events: Time-bound entities with privacy, capacity, ticketing, and delayed location reveal
reservationLogic: Host-accepted reveal of location via locationRevealedAt

capabilities: Platform fee split via Stripe payment intents or checkout sessions

trustAndSafety.csamCurrentState: Fail-safe placeholder; blocks uploads in production if scanner is not configured.

architecture.actualShape: Modular monolith centered in apps/web, inside a monorepo with future-facing service boundaries.

responsibilitySplit.apps: User-facing frontends and current BFF implementation
responsibilitySplit.packages: Shared libraries for DB, UI tokens, config, and minimal core typing
responsibilitySplit.services: Intended future backend separation, currently mostly stubs

dominantPatterns: Monorepo modularization by platform and shared package
Feature gating via VIP flags and entitlements table
Route-based modularity over formal MVC/Clean Architecture layers
notClearlyPresent: Strong DDD tactical modeling in packages/core

criticalStructuralTension.name: Dual DB schema divergence
criticalStructuralTension.impact: Potential drift between runtime typing/queries and generated migrations.

apps.mobile: Expo app with auth guard and placeholder tabs/routes
apps.web: Primary implemented surface: UI, API routes, webhooks, and most business logic

packages.core: Minimal shared domain package; currently mostly AccountType

eslint.consolePolicy: warn, with allowlist for warn and error

notablyMissing: Photo moderation and CSAM flow tests

notes: Most authenticated routes resolve Clerk session first, then lookup internal user via users.clerkUserId.
There is a mix of Drizzle query builder usage and raw SQL via (db as any).execute(sql...).
apps/web is the effective API layer of the system.

endToEndFlows.name: Onboarding and initial activation
majorSteps: User selects account type, age, display name, and optional approximate location
Client sends PATCH /api/users/me
Client redirects to kink test, club setup, or shop setup
endToEndFlows.backendReality: PATCH /api/users/me currently only updates display_name, leaving onboardingComplete/accountType/dateOfBirth persistence incomplete.
risks: UI intent and backend persistence diverge

endToEndFlows.name: Kink test to compatibility-ranked feed
majorSteps: Client posts quiz answers to /api/quiz
Backend derives tags and archetype and inserts quizResults
Feed loads candidates via /api/feed
VIP users receive ranking through rankCandidates()
businessRules: Free users have daily match cap

endToEndFlows.name: Mutual match to encrypted messaging
majorSteps: Client loads mutual matches via /api/matches
Thread is created or resolved via /api/threads
Messages are loaded from /api/messages
Outgoing plaintext is encrypted client-side and persisted via /api/messages
Realtime delivery happens over Ably
endToEndFlows.securityModel: Server stores ciphertext; private key remains client-side.

endToEndFlows.name: Verification workflow
majorSteps: Client gets upload URL from /api/verifications/photo-upload
Client uploads evidence directly to storage
Client creates pending verification via /api/verifications
Admin reviews via /api/admin/verifications and PATCH /api/admin/verifications/[id]

endToEndFlows.name: Photos to moderation and CSAM screening
majorSteps: Client may persist via /api/photos or confirm through /api/photos/confirm
Confirmed flow fetches uploaded object and runs scanImageForCSAM()
Unsafe objects are deleted and reported
Human moderation later updates moderationStatus via admin routes
risks: Admin rejection path may pass a URL instead of object key to storage delete helper

endToEndFlows.name: Events and delayed location reveal
majorSteps: Host creates event via /api/events
Public readers load event detail from /api/events/[id] with customLocation nulled
Attendees reserve via /api/reservations
Host accepts via /api/reservations/[id]/accept, which sets locationRevealedAt and notifies user
businessRules: Capacity can move users to waitlist
VIP users get higher reservation priority
Exact location is hidden before acceptance

endToEndFlows.name: Seller onboarding to checkout
majorSteps: Seller starts Stripe Connect via /api/connect/onboard
Seller status is synced through /api/connect/status
Seller creates moderated products via /api/products
Buyer creates order via /api/orders
Buyer is redirected to Stripe Checkout through /api/orders/checkout-session
Stripe webhook marks order as paid
businessRules: Only SEX_SHOP accounts can create products
Shop payoutsEnabled is required before listing products
Platform fee is derived from MARKETPLACE_DEFAULT_COMMISSION_BPS

endToEndFlows.name: VIP upgrade to entitlements
majorSteps: Client starts Stripe subscription checkout via /api/stripe/checkout
Stripe checkout and subscription webhooks update subscriptions
users.isVip and subscriptionTier are updated
VIP entitlements are activated or deactivated
endToEndFlows.outcome: Unlocks compatibility ranking and removes daily usage limits in product logic.

environment.note: The central env schema does not fully enumerate all environment variables actually used throughout the codebase.

strengths: Clear product vision and domain boundaries
Billing, storage, push, auth, and webhook integrations already modeled
materialGaps: apps/web still concentrates most backend responsibilities
Dual DB schemas create migration/runtime drift risk
Mobile/admin/services lag far behind the web app
Clerk webhook validates signatures but does not sync users
maturityAndRisks.practicalInterpretation: Treat apps/web as the current source of operational truth. Be careful with data model assumptions because the runtime schema and migration schema diverge.
